title: orobanche purpurea f. millefolii
intro: Orobanche purpurea f. millefolii is a rare and unique subspecies of the Orobanche purpurea plant, also known as the purple broomrape, a type of parasitic plant that derives its nutrients from the roots of nearby host plants, typically those of the Achillea millefolium (yarrow). The medicinal properties of Orobanche purpurea f. millefolii include its potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which may contribute to its traditional use in treating various health conditions. The health benefits associated with this plant include its possible application in reducing fever, alleviating respiratory issues, and supporting the immune system. In herbal preparations, Orobanche purpurea f. millefolii is often used in the form of teas, tinctures, or infusions, although its availability and usage may be limited due to its rarity and the need for further research on its efficacy and safety.
